access_hardware_computer: [CORE] A computer is an electronic device that processes data and runs programs, typically with a screen, keyboard, and mouse. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work is designed to run on a desktop or laptop system and may require precise cursor control, large displays, or locally executed software.
access_hardware_printer: [CORE] A printer is a device that produces physical output from digital files, usually on paper. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes printable components, such as generated poems, visual layouts, or instructions meant to exist as physical objects.
access_hardware_internet_browser: [CORE] An internet browser is a software application used to access and display websites and online content. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work is accessed through web pages and relies on web technologies such as hyperlinks, scripts, or online media.
access_hardware_loudspeakers: [CORE] Loudspeakers are devices that convert electrical signals into audible sound in a shared space. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes sound meant to be heard publicly, such as spoken text, sound poetry, or ambient audio in an installation.
access_hardware_headphones: [CORE] Headphones are wearable audio devices that allow private listening. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes audio intended for individual and immersive listening, such as whispered narration or spatial sound.
access_hardware_smartphone: [CORE] A smartphone is a handheld computing device with a touchscreen and built-in sensors. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work is designed for mobile use and may involve touch gestures, movement, or location-based interaction.
access_hardware_camera: [CORE] A camera is a device that captures images or video from the physical environment. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work incorporates live visual input from the reader's surroundings as part of the literary experience.
access_hardware_tablet: [CORE] A tablet is a portable touchscreen computer larger than a smartphone but smaller than a laptop. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work is designed for touch-based interaction and visual reading on a medium-sized portable screen.
access_hardware_cd_player: [CORE] A compact disc player is a device that reads digital content stored on optical discs. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work is distributed on physical digital media and accessed without needing an internet connection.
access_hardware_motion_capture: [CORE] Motion capture systems detect and track human body movement using sensors or cameras. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work responds to the reader's physical gestures, making bodily movement part of the interaction.
access_hardware_no_information: [CORE] No information indicates missing technical documentation. [INDICATOR] This indicates the access hardware used is unknown.
publication_type_application: [CORE] An application is a self-contained program designed to perform specific functions on a device. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work is experienced through a dedicated executable program rather than through a browser or static file.
publication_type_file: [CORE] A file is a discrete unit of stored digital data, such as a document, image, or executable. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work exists as a downloadable or transferable object that can be opened directly.
publication_type_website: [CORE] A website is a collection of interconnected web pages accessible through the internet. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work is structured as an online environment navigated through links and web interfaces.
publication_type_platform: [CORE] A platform is an online service that hosts and organizes user content and interaction. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work exists within a larger system such as a social network or hosting service rather than independently.
publication_type_cd/dvd: [CORE] A compact disc or digital versatile disc is a physical storage medium used to distribute digital data. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work was distributed as a physical digital object requiring specific hardware to access.
publication_type_exhibition: [CORE] An exhibition is a curated public presentation of works in a physical or virtual space. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work is presented as part of a curated display, often with spatial or contextual framing.
publication_type_print: [CORE] Print refers to content produced on physical materials such as paper. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes a physical textual or visual component, even if it originates digitally.
publication_type_installation: [CORE] An installation is a spatial artwork designed to be experienced in a specific physical environment. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work involves physical arrangement, space, and possibly sensors or projection.
publication_type_software: [CORE] Software is a set of instructions that enables a computer to perform specific tasks. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work exists as programmable logic executed by a computer system.
publication_type_social_media: [CORE] Social media are online services where users create and share content with others. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work exists within interactive social systems such as message feeds or public posts.
publication_type_virtual_world: [CORE] A virtual world is a persistent digital environment that simulates space and interaction. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work takes place in an immersive simulated environment where readers may navigate and interact spatially.
publication_type_no_information: [CORE] No information indicates missing technical documentation. [INDICATOR] This indicates the publication type is unknown.
genre_poetry: [CORE] Poetry is a literary form that emphasizes rhythm, sound, and condensed language. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work focuses on expressive language, structure, or visual arrangement rather than linear storytelling.
genre_narrative: [CORE] Narrative is a literary form that presents events in sequence to tell a story. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work focuses on characters, events, and progression over time.
genre_poetry_and_narrative: [CORE] Poetry and narrative combines expressive language with storytelling elements. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes both structured poetic language and story progression.
genre_other: [CORE] Other refers to literary forms that do not fit traditional poetry or narrative categories. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work may be experimental, procedural, or conceptual rather than conventional literary forms.
genre_no_information: [CORE] No information indicates missing documentation. [INDICATOR] This indicates the genre is unknown.
technical_requirements_internet_connecti: [CORE] An internet connection enables communication between a device and remote servers. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work depends on remote data, online interaction, or web-based delivery.
technical_requirements_install_software/: [CORE] Installing software or add-ons involves adding programs to a system to enable functionality. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work requires specific software components beyond default system capabilities.
technical_requirements_internet_browser: [CORE] An internet browser is a program used to access and display web content. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work runs within a browser environment rather than as standalone software.
technical_requirements_ram: [CORE] Random access memory is temporary electronic storage used while a computer is running programs. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work requires sufficient memory resources to operate correctly.
technical_requirements_execute_locally: [CORE] Executing locally means running directly on a user's device without internet communication. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work exists as locally stored software or files.
technical_requirements_submit_to_emulati: [CORE] Emulation involves recreating older hardware or software environments on modern systems. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work depends on obsolete systems and must be simulated to function.
technical_requirements_access_to_platfor: [CORE] Access to a platform or service means using a specific hosted system to reach content. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work is dependent on external infrastructure controlled by a provider.
technical_requirements_no_information: [CORE] No information indicates missing technical documentation. [INDICATOR] This indicates the technical requirements are unknown.
format_image: [CORE] An image is a visual representation composed of pixels or graphical elements. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work presents meaning primarily through visual composition.
format_video_game: [CORE] A video game is an interactive digital system involving player input and visual feedback. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes gameplay mechanics such as movement, exploration, or rule-based interaction.
format_text: [CORE] Text is written language composed of words and symbols. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work communicates meaning primarily through written language.
format_virtual_environment: [CORE] A virtual environment is a digitally simulated space that can be explored. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work involves spatial navigation within a simulated world.
format_video: [CORE] Video is a sequence of moving images displayed over time. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes temporal visual sequences such as recorded or generated motion.
format_audio: [CORE] Audio is sound recorded or generated electronically. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work communicates meaning through spoken language, music, or sound.
format_database: [CORE] A database is an organized collection of structured data. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work draws from structured information sets or allows querying of stored elements.
format_search_engine: [CORE] A search engine is a system that retrieves information based on queries. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work involves querying, filtering, or retrieving textual or media content.
format_physical_artefact: [CORE] A physical artifact is a tangible object existing in material form. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes physical components alongside digital processes.
format_access_to_platform/service: [CORE] Access to a platform or service means interacting through hosted online infrastructure. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work depends on external systems rather than existing independently.
format_no_information: [CORE]  No information indicates missing technical documentation. [INDICATOR] This indicates the format is unknown.
reading_process_navigation: [CORE] Navigation involves moving between different elements or locations. [INDICATOR] This indicates the reader progresses by choosing paths or links.
reading_process_manipulation: [CORE] Manipulation involves physically altering digital elements through input. [INDICATOR] This indicates the reader directly changes visual or textual elements.
reading_process_observation: [CORE] Observation involves viewing content without altering it. [INDICATOR] This indicates the reader primarily watches or listens rather than intervening.
reading_process_alter_elements: [CORE] Altering elements involves modifying existing components. [INDICATOR] This indicates the reader actively changes the state of the work.
reading_process_content_generation: [CORE] Content generation involves producing new material. [INDICATOR] This indicates the reader triggers creation of new text or media.
reading_process_activate_/_deactivate: [CORE] Activating or deactivating involves turning functions on or off. [INDICATOR] This indicates the reader controls system features.
reading_process_detect_input_devices: [CORE] Detecting input devices involves recognizing connected hardware. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work responds differently based on hardware.
reading_process_selection_of_elements: [CORE] Selection involves choosing among available options. [INDICATOR] This indicates the reader determines progression through choices.
reading_process_log_in: [CORE] Logging in involves authenticating identity in a system. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes user-specific content or interaction.
reading_process_upload_files: [CORE] Uploading files involves sending local data to a system. [INDICATOR] This indicates the reader contributes external content.
reading_process_download_content: [CORE] Downloading involves transferring data from a remote system. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work provides downloadable components.
reading_process_input_information: [CORE] Inputting information involves entering data manually. [INDICATOR] This indicates the reader contributes textual or numerical input.
reading_process_no_information: [CORE] No information indicates missing technical documentation. [INDICATOR] This indicates the publication type is unknown.
program_adobe_acrobat: [CORE] Adobe Acrobat is software used to read and display portable document files. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work exists as structured document files requiring a document reader.
program_bitsy: [CORE] Bitsy is a tool for creating small interactive games and narratives. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work uses simple visual game mechanics for storytelling.
program_bot: [CORE] A bot is an automated software agent that performs tasks. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work operates through automated responses or generation.
program_qr_code: [CORE] A quick response code is a visual pattern readable by devices. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work links physical and digital access through scanning.
program_exe: [CORE] An executable file is a program that runs directly on a computer. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work is distributed as runnable software.
program_flash: [CORE] Flash was software used to create interactive multimedia content. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work uses timeline-based animation and interaction.
program_gif: [CORE] A graphics interchange format file supports animated images. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work uses looping visual sequences.
program_html/php: [CORE] Hypertext markup language and personal home page are technologies for building web pages. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work is built using web programming.
program_artificial_intelligence: [CORE] Artificial intelligence refers to computational systems that simulate intelligent processes. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work generates or analyzes content automatically.
program_java: [CORE] Java is a programming language used to create software applications. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work is implemented through structured programming.
program_macromedia: [CORE] Macromedia developed multimedia authoring tools. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work was created using multimedia authoring software.
program_microsoft_office: [CORE] Microsoft Office is software for creating documents and presentations. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work uses structured document formats.
program_midipoet: [CORE] Midipoet is software for generating poetry using structured rules. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work involves automated poetic generation.
program_does_not_apply: [CORE] Does not apply indicates no specific program was used. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work does not depend on identifiable software.
program_eko_platform: [CORE] Eko platform is a system for interactive storytelling. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes branching narrative interaction.
program_no_information: [CORE] No information indicates missing technical documentation. [INDICATOR] This indicates the program used is unknown.
program_video: [CORE] Video refers to moving image recording technology. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work involves recorded visual sequences.
program_virtual_reality_modelling_langua: [CORE] Virtual reality modelling language defines three-dimensional environments. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes navigable spatial environments.
program_ascii: [CORE] ASCII is a system for representing text characters numerically. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work uses text as symbolic computational material.
program_minitel: [CORE] Minitel was a networked information system. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work exists in early networked text environments.
program_fortran: [CORE] Fortran is a programming language designed for computation. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work involves structured computational logic.
program_unity: [CORE] Unity is software for creating interactive environments. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes spatial interaction and simulation.
program_jgnoetry: [CORE] jGnoetry is software for automated poetry generation. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work produces text algorithmically.
program_asymetrix_toolbox: [CORE] Asymetrix Toolbox was multimedia authoring software. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work was assembled using multimedia authoring tools.
program_jasc_animation_shop: [CORE] Jasc Animation Shop was software for creating animated images. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes frame-based animation.
program_managana: [CORE] Managana is software for creating interactive narratives. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work combines media and interaction.
program_paint_shop_pro: [CORE] Paint Shop Pro is software for creating and editing images. [INDICATOR] This indicates the work includes digitally created visual imagery.
